learning_outcomes.description: Mitigate project risks by communicating progress, contributing equitably, meeting milestones, and maintaining accountability throughout the duration of a team-based mechanical design project.

Generate, evaluate, and select design solutions that meet prescribed system requirements using structured approaches, such as morphological analysis, concept scoring matrices, and design for manufacturability principles.

Identify and apply regulations, engineering standards, and industry codes pertaining to safety, design documentation, and design practice throughout the detailed implementation of the mechanical system design.

Integrate sustainability principles into design decisions by selecting low environmental-impact materials, designing for maintenance and disassembly, and selecting manufacturing processes that balance performance, cost, and resource efficiency.

Apply Computer Aided Design (CAD) tools to create parametric three-dimensional models of mechanical components and assemblies, and Finite Element Analysis (FEA) tools predict structural response to loads and optimise component geometry/assembly layout.

Apply Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) methods and tools throughout the system design process to manage design data, maintain version control, facilitate collaborative workflows, and ensure design traceability from requirements through to manufacturing documentation.

Build a functional system prototype using the facilities and safe working procedures of an engineering makerspace and perform verification and validation tests to confirm that the prototype meets design and safety requirements.

Effectively communicate design decisions, analysis results, verification and validation outcomes, and manufacturing documentation through technical reports, presentations, and technical drawings that meet industry standards for documentation and communication.

assessments.task_description:
Teams will generate, evaluate, and select conceptual design solutions that meet prescribed system requirements using structured approaches. The conceptual design stage will be guided by principles of sustainability, manufacturability, and maintainability.

Teams will demonstrate their ability to communicate conceptual design choices as a slide deck. Team members will demonstrate equitable contribution by leading the conceptual design stage of their allocated subsystem and preparing the relevant component of the assessment submission.


If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission requirements and marking rubric provided on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Teams will perform detailed design of their proposed system solution using CAD, FEA, and PLM tools. Teams will identify and apply engineering standards and design practices throughout the detailed implementation of the mechanical system design. Teams will refine their conceptual design choices to ensure the performance, manufacturability, and maintainability of the system.

Teams will deliver a complete digital model of their proposed mechanical system, an engineering drawing package, and a Bill of Materials. Teams will finalise the materials and commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) parts needed for prototype construction.

Teams will demonstrate their ability to communicate their manufacturing documentation as a set of technical drawings, the costing of COTS parts and materials, and a fabrication/manufacturing plan. Team members will demonstrate equitable contribution by leading the detailed design stage of their allocated subsystem and preparing the relevant component of the assessment submission.


If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Teams will verify a prescribed subset of system requirements in the digital environment and provide an update on the fabrication progress.

Teams will deliver a technical report on the analyses undertaken using CAD and FEA tools to digitally verify a prescribed subset of system requirements. Teams will provide a progress update on the project against the prescribed project schedule and identify project completion risks and control measures.

Team members will demonstrate equitable contribution by leading the detailed design stage of their allocated subsystem and preparing the relevant component of the assessment submission.


If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

assessments.assessment_title: Performance Validation, Design Handover, and Disassembly
assessments.due_date:
Performance Validation - 20% Week 11 Mon - Week 13 Thu

Design Handover - 30% Week 13 Fri - Week 13 Fri

Disassembly Workshop - 5% Exam week 1 Mon - Exam week 2 Fri
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Performance, Product/ Design
assessments.task_description:
Performance Validation - 20%

Teams will collaborate in the engineering makerspace and laboratory space to fabricate and test a physical prototype of their mechanical system design. The teams will verify that the prototype meets the system requirements. The teams will validate the system performance against the prescribed targets. Teams will receive multiple supervised testing opportunities from Week 11 to Week 13 and a testing schedule will be communicated via Blackboard.

Design Handover - 30%

Teams will hand over their prototype system to the teaching staff upon completion of performance testing in Week 13. Teams must hand over the system without modifying or removing any components that were part of the system during performance testing. Teams will also hand over up-to-date digital design data in the PLM environment (formal revision workflows in PLM to reflect any design changes made since the detailed design review assessment, up-to-date CAD replica of the physical prototype).

Teams will also submit a formal report on Blackboard providing: (1) an as-built Manufacturing Bill of Materials that identifies the quantity, source, and cost of every component/part on the prototype, (2) tracing each UQ-fabricated part from the final product to its manufacturing drawing, source material, and fabrication process, (3) a complete and up to date engineering drawing package comprising of all assemblies, subassemblies, and components/parts (revised from the original drawing package submission from Assessment 3). An Excel spreadsheet for UQ card expenditure and receipts to also be uploaded on Blackboard as part of the same submission.

Disassembly Workshop - 5%

During the examination periods, teams will return to disassemble their prototype systems using basic hand tools. Disassembly workshop time and venue will be communicated to students after the release of the examination timetable. The teaching staff will assess the completeness of the disassembly and the reusability of the COTS parts and components.


If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission requirements and marking rubric are provided on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
All students must formally meet with their mentor (an academic staff or delegated casual demonstrator) once a week during the weekly studio sessions. In these meetings, students must engage in discussion with their mentors and actively show evidence of progress towards assessment deliverables. The mentors will evaluate the degree of participation based on progress updates from students and the evidence of their individual progress presented in-person.

Participation requirements and mentor evaluation rubric are provided on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Each student must provide a written progress statement to their team’s project coordinator on a weekly basis, following a prescribed format made available on Blackboard. The team’s project coordinator shall compile the statements and submit these to Blackboard prior to each studio session. The compiled progress update should explain how each student in a team participated adequately in the project work by progressing towards the assessment deliverables since the previous week’s discussion.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Studio sessions

During the studio sessions, you will collaborate with your team members to work on the assessment deliverables. Teaching staff and casual demonstrators will provide guidance on your design choices, project progress, and appropriate use of digital and physical tools and facilities.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Studio sessions During the studio sessions, you will collaborate with your team members to work on the assessment deliverables. Teaching staff and casual demonstrators will provide guidance on your design choices, project progress, and appropriate use of digital and physical tools and facilities.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Workshop sessions

During workshops, you will contribute to Communities of Practice formed around a set of defined team roles. The workshops will mainly involve peer-based learning of effective strategies to perform the various design and coordination roles. Teaching staff and casual demonstrators will facilitate discussions and activities in several parallel streams, corresponding to the different defined roles.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L08

The workshops will mainly involve peer-based learning of effective strategies to perform the various design and coordination roles. Teaching staff and casual demonstrators will facilitate discussions and activities in several parallel streams, corresponding to the different defined roles.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 7 To Week 13(07 Sep - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Fabrication and performance testing

Your team will fabricate and assemble a prototype of your designed system using the tools, facilities, and services available in UQ Innovate. You will perform testing to validate your system prototype performance against the specifications defined in the project brief.


Learning outcomes: L01, L06, L07, L08

You will perform testing to validate your system prototype performance against the specifications defined in the project brief.
